steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: Transition question. The first sentence describes majoritarian elections producing two-party systems; the second describes proportional-representation systems producing multi-partyism. The two electoral systems yield opposite outcomes, so the transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Subsequently,’: signals time sequence/causation (one event following another). The two sentences describe parallel-existing systems, not a sequence of events. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Conversely,’: signals direct contrast between two opposing situations — exactly the relationship between majoritarian two-party outcomes and PR multi-party outcomes. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘For instance,’: signals exemplification. The second sentence is not an example of the first; it describes a different electoral system with a different result. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In other words,’: signals restatement of the same idea. The second sentence introduces a contrasting system, not a paraphrase. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
